data.title: Earnz Unit to Acquire A&D in GBP9 Million Deal
data.description:
Earnz plc subsidiary Earnz Holdings agreed to acquire A&D from the target's shareholders for up to 2.8 million pounds sterling.

The British energy services group unit will make the payment through a combination of cash and shares and will partly be based on A&D's achievement of certain performance targets, according to a Thursday release.

Deal closing is subject to shareholder approval at the company's general meeting on June 30, among other conditions.

To view the announcement in full, please click on the associated attachment

data.title: Taiwan's FSC Amends Rules to Curb Hostile Takeovers with Stricter Control Rules
data.description:
Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission will tighten and change rules to strengthen oversight of financial holding companies' investment activities, according to a Tuesday announcement by the regulator.

Under the proposal, hostile takeovers will be restricted unless companies can show they'll gain majority control or board backing. The draft also calls for tougher public acquisition terms, including mandatory cash payments and a higher minimum stake of over 25%.

The regulator will require first-time investments to be reviewed by an audit committee with input from independent experts. Additionally, public acquisition terms cannot be disclosed before FSC approval, and rejected investment plans cannot be resubmitted within a year.

The draft is open for public comment and will be published in the Executive Yuan Gazette and on the FSC website, it said.

data.title: Amaroq Minerals Raises GBP45 Million via Share Placing
data.description:
Amaroq Minerals (, AMRQ.IC) raised 45 million pounds sterling in gross proceeds through an oversubscribed share placing with existing and new investors.

The mine development company conditionally placed 52,986,036 new shares at 0.85 pound apiece in the transaction, according to a Thursday release. The shares are expected to start trading on the London bourse's AIM segment, the Icelandic Exchange and Canada's TSX-V on June 30.

Net proceeds will be used for commissioning and production expansion at its Nalunaq project in Greenland, to create a new hub in West Greenland, for exploration drilling and technical studies of the assets, as well as to provide additional balance sheet strength and working capital.

data.title: Aktia Pankki CEO Steps Down, Successor Named
data.description:
Aktia Pankki Chief Executive Officer Aleksi Lehtonen agreed to step down on Thursday, according to a same-day filing.

The bank named Carl Haglund as its new CEO, with a joining date expected no later than September. The bank's board also appointed Anssi Huhta, current executive vice president of banking business, as interim CEO until Haglund's joining.

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Gaining
data.description:
Stocks on the rise in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0838 GMT - BP has scope to lower capital expenditure in a down-cycle, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. They write this after hosting a virtual call with BP's Chief Executive Murray Auchincloss. BP is currently guiding for capex of $14.5 billion in 2025, of which $13.8 billion is organic spend--which includes spending in existing operations--, they add. BP could remove around $500 million from its exploration budget, around $1 billion from major projects that have not yet been sanctioned, and has flexibility in its U.S. onshore business where annual capex is around $2.4 billion, they write. Shares trade up 1.6% at 379.75 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0816 GMT - Halma's strong performance in fiscal 2025 offer continued confidence in future growth, IG analysts say in a research note. The U.K. engineering group saw record results for the 12 months, achieving its 22nd consecutive year of profit growth with an 11% rise in revenue and a 16% increase in adjusted pretax profit. A strong performance across all sectors and regions, as well as a robust cash generation, and sustained investment that supported a 7% dividend increase signals confidence moving forward, they say. Shares trade 6.4% higher at 32.14 pounds and are leading the FTSE 100 index. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Shares in gold miners trade up as futures in the precious metal are boosted by safe-haven demand. Gold futures trade up 0.6% at $3,364.20 a troy ounce, having risen as high as $3,398.40/oz earlier in the session. Investor appetite for gold has been boosted by geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East. In South Africa, AngloGold Ashanti rises 2.5%, Gold Fields is up 2.2% while Harmony Gold rises 1.1%. In London, Endeavour Mining rises 0.8% while gold and silver miner Fresnillo rises 0.3%.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0813 GMT - Halma's guidance for fiscal 2026 looks very strong on organic growth, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. The U.K. engineering group is guiding for upper single-digit organic growth but a higher foreign-exchange rate headwind is likely to leave consensus forecasts broadly unchanged, the analysts say. The beat and the organic guidance supported by further growth in photonics should drives shares on Thursday, they say. Shares trade 7.6% higher at 32.50 pounds, leading the FTSE 100 index risers. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0800 GMT - BP could announce a flurry of disposals in the second half of the year, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. They write this after hosting a virtual call with BP's Chief Executive Murray Auchincloss. In the near term, the focus is on disposing infrastructure assets and some deals could be announced relatively soon, they say. Work on the Gelsenkirken refinery disposal continues while the Lightsource process is due to launch imminently, they add. Overall, BP is confident it can reach its $20 billion disposal target, the analysts write. Shares trade up 1.7% at 380 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0746 GMT - Crest Nicholson's new strategy is working as it posts a reassuring set of 1H results, RBC Capital Markets analysts Anthony Codling and Oliver Dyson say in a note. Its current performance is in line with views and the outlook seems more calm than stormy, the analysts say. The housebuilder is better organized and better placed to cope with changeable housing market conditions after addressing legacy performance issues and is rebuilding a profitable and sustainable business, the analysts say. "Crest is well placed to benefit from improving market conditions and as the stars align on planning and funding for social and affordable housing, Crest is on course to deliver on its medium-term guidance" the analysts say. Shares are up 0.5% at 190.20 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0734 GMT - Tesco's dominance in the British supermarket sector is undiminished, Interactive Investor's Richard Hunter writes in a note. For the first quarter of fiscal 2026, the U.K. grocer booked an increase in revenue of 4.6% on a like-for-like basis to 16.38 billion pounds. Tesco's current market share of 28% is more than that of its two nearest rivals, Sainsbury and Asda, combined, with 15% and 12%, respectively. Tesco has managed to lower prices in a highly competitive sector and against higher costs including national insurance contributions, Hunter says. Shares trade 0.7% higher at 388 pence. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - Tesco has managed to gain market share in recent years on a strong performance, RBC Capital Markets analysts Manjari Dhar and Richard Chamberlain say in a note. The British grocer booked 16.38 billion pounds in group sales excluding value-added tax and fuel for the 13 weeks ended May 24. Like-for-like sales rose 4.6% over the period. Tesco seems a best-in-class player in the U.K. food retail space, they say. The company has a strong business model and an experienced management team, the analysts add. However, a step-up in competitiveness by some key peers could mean that further gains are more difficult to come by, they say. Shares are up 0.6% at 387.8 pence. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: Tesco CEO: Commitment Is to Maintain Good Momentum
BP Has Flexibility to Reduce Capital Expenditure — Market Talk
data.description:
0838 GMT - BP has scope to lower capital expenditure in a down-cycle, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. They write this after hosting a virtual call with BP's Chief Executive Murray Auchincloss. BP is currently guiding for capex of $14.5 billion in 2025, of which $13.8 billion is organic spend--which includes spending in existing operations--, they add. BP could remove around $500 million from its exploration budget, around $1 billion from major projects that have not yet been sanctioned, and has flexibility in its U.S. onshore business where annual capex is around $2.4 billion, they write. Shares trade up 1.6% at 379.75 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - Oracle shares are climbing Thursday premarket after the cloud-services company posted strong results for the last quarter of fiscal 2025 and said fiscal 2026 would be even better. Oracle stock closed 0.6% lower at $176.38 Wednesday ahead of results. Shares are up 8.1% premarket, touching a high of $191.00. Revenue grew 11% to $15.90 billion in the three months ended May 31, above a FactSet projection of $15.57 billion. Earnings per share of $1.19 also exceeded a FactSet forecast of $1.16. Chief Executive Safra Catz said fiscal 2025 was a very good year, but fiscal 2026 will be even better. The executive expects Oracle's total cloud growth rate--applications plus infrastructure--to increase to more than 40% in fiscal 2026 from 24% in fiscal 2025. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - Companies are delaying, but not cancelling, dealmaking decisions since President Trump unveiled his plans to impose sweeping tariffs at the beginning of April, Deutsche Bank CEO Christian Sewing says at a Goldman Sachs conference. Some deals the German bank is working on won't be accounted for in the second quarter, but slip into the third, Sewing says. Overall, the performance of Deutsche Bank's investment bank in the second quarter will be in line with the year-earlier period, Sewing says. A weaker-than-expected performance in its origination-and-advisory activities will be offset by its fixed income-and-currencies and its financing operations, he says. Deutsche Bank shares fall 1.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: GameStop falls following $1.75 bln convertible debt offering
data.description:
** Shares of videogame retailer GameStop fall 12.47% premarket, after falling 5.31% in the previous session

** Co announces private offering of $1.75 billion 0% 7-yr convertible bonds as it looks to raise capital

** Co says it plans to use the offering’s net proceeds for general corporate needs, including investments "in a manner consistent with GameStop’s Investment Policy" and potential acquisitions

** Earlier this week, GME reported Q1 rev down 17% to $732.4 million vs $881.8 million a year ago, hurt by a consumer shift to digital gaming

** Stock down ~9% YTD through Wednesday

data.title: Tesco Executives Spoke During a Call After Results
Rising Geopolitical Tension, U.S. Trade Policy Concerns Weigh on Global Markets
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Stock markets had a muted start to Thursday, with U.S. stock futures down a touch, a narrowly mixed close in Asia and a weaker open across European markets.

Elements driving risk-off sentiment included rising geopolitical tension and continuing concerns around U.S. trade policy. U.S. Treasury yields were slightly down extending Wednesday's falls after the latest monthly report on consumer prices. The dollar also continued to fall.

  • U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were recently down 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased 0.3%, as did Nasdaq futures.
  • Stock markets in Asia closed mixed, with Japan's Nikkei 225 index falling 0.6% and Hong Kong's Hang Seng ending down 1.1%, whereas China's benchmark Shanghai Composite held steady and South Korea's Kospi index closed up 0.4%.
  • In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 fell 0.4% in morning trading as major bourses eased at the open. Outliers were oil stocks, boosted by gains in crude benchmarks on Wednesday due to rising geopolitical fears in the Middle East. In London, BP was up 1.7% in early trade, Shell rose 1.3% and France's TotalEnergies was up 1.9%. Norway's Equinor climbed 1.7%.
  • Oil prices were slipping early in Europe after jumping more than 4% in the previous trading session. Brent crude was down 1.2% to $68.93 a barrel, while WTI fell 1.1% to $67.39 a barrel.
  • The dollar was recently down to seven-week lows against a basket of currencies and against the euro, extending the previous day's drop after lower-than-forecast U.S. inflation data that increase prospects of the U.S. Federal Reserve cutting interest rates.

The DXY dollar index fell to a low of 98.247, while the euro hit a high of $1.1532, according to LSEG. Bitcoin was 1.3% lower in line with weakening risk appetite.

  • The U.S. Treasury's auction of $22 billion of 30-year bonds is in focus Thursday after the U.S. CPI data Wednesday boosted bonds. Ahead of that, the 30-year Treasury yield was last down about one basis point at 4.903%, according to Tradeweb. The 10-year Treasury yield fells 0.6 basis point to 4.408%.
  • Gold futures were up on safe-haven demand, rising 0.6% to $3,364.60 a troy ounce, having risen as high as $3,398.40/oz earlier in the session.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Unit Launches Debt Offering
data.description:
Canadian Pacific Kansas City said late Wednesday that its Canadian Pacific Railway unit is offering 1.4 billion Canadian dollars ($1.02 billion) of notes in three tranches.

The offering comprises CA$500 million of 4% notes due 2032, CA$600 million of 4.4% notes due 2036, and CA$300 million of 4.8% notes due 2055.

The company expects the offering to close Friday and plans to use the net proceeds to refinance debt and for general corporate expenditures.

data.title: Enbee Trade Forms Strategic Alliance With Sunrise FinServe to Boost Lending
data.description:
Enbee Trade and Finance entered a strategic alliance with Sunrise FinServe to enhance its loan disbursement capabilities, according to a Wednesday filing.

The partnership aims to streamline loan processing, improve customer experience, and expand financial inclusion across Maharashtra and Gujarat by leveraging Sunrise's network and lending expertise, the filing said.

data.title: Aegis Logistics Arm Commission Cryogenic LPG Terminal at Mangalore, India
data.description:
Sea Lord Containers, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Aegis Logistics (NSE:AEGISCHEM, NSE:AEGISCHEM), has commissioned its cryogenic liquified petroleum gas (LPG) terminal at Mangalore, India, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The terminal will be used for storage and terminalling of liquified petroleum gases with a static storage capacity of 82,000 MT. It has been developed by Sea Lord on behalf of its associated company, Aegis Vopak Terminals.

The terminal will subsequently be transferred to Aegis Vopak Terminals.

data.title: Calavo Growers Receives Acquisition Proposal
data.description:
Calavo Growers said late Wednesday it received a non-binding proposal to acquire all of its outstanding shares at $32 per share.

The offer includes a mix of cash and stock from the proposing party, subject to due diligence and financing conditions, the company said.

Calavo also said its board is reviewing the proposal with input from its legal and financial advisers.

data.title: Leonardo Being Chosen by Airbus Will Boost Its Troubled Aerostructures Unit — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo being awarded the production of the rear section of the fuselage for the Airbus A220 will help the Italian group's troubled Aerostructures division to approach break-even, Equita analyst Martino De Ambroggi says. Valerio Moro, the new chief executive of Airbus Italy, told reporters that Leonardo had been chosen for the task, and that the work will start after the summer, IlSole24 newspaper reports. The aerospace and defense company's unit has posted triple-digit losses for seven years. Supplying those parts to Airbus will be a long-term program and will help Leonardo crafting a deal to rescue the Aerostructures unit with a financial or industrial partner, De Ambroggi says. Leonardo's shares are down 0.8% at 47.96 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Shanghai Junshi Biosciences' Unit Received Acceptance Notice Issued By NMPA
data.description:
Shanghai Junshi Biosciences Co Ltd :

  • JT118 RECEIVED ACCEPTANCE NOTICE FOR INVESTIGATIONAL NEW DRUG APPLICATION

data.title: CMC Magnetics Sells Stake in Dynapack International Worth NT$464 Million
data.description:
CMC Magnetics disposed of 2,378,000 shares of Dynapack International Technology (TWO:3211) for NT$464.2 million between May 26 and June 11 at an average price of NT$195.22 per share.

The shares were sold for portfolio management purposes, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: CSX Says Locomotive Engineers, Trainmen Union Ratifies Wage Deal
data.description:
CSX said late Wednesday employees represented by the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen voted to ratify a five-year collective bargaining agreement covering around 3,400 locomotive engineers.

CSX said the deal has similar wage, health and welfare upgrades as its agreements with 13 other unions.

The union said the agreement, which is valid until the end of 2029, was approved by 53.6% of members who submitted ballots. It includes compounded wage increases of 18.77%, the union said.
